claimText: The Wild Yak (Bos mutus) is native to China and India but is considered regionally extinct in Bhutan.
evidenceExcerpt: Wild Yak (Bos mutus) is native to China and India but is regionally extinct from Bhutan.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of the Wild Yak.

claimText: The Wild Yak is classified as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List, protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA), and listed in CMS Appendix I.
evidenceExcerpt: Wild Yak conservation status: IUCN Vulnerable (VU), WPA Schedule I, CMS Appendix I.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the conservation status of Indian wildlife.

claimText: The Domestic Yak (Bos grunniens), known as the 'ship of the Himalayas', adapts to high altitudes by possessing larger lungs and a larger heart compared to cattle.
evidenceExcerpt: Domestic Yak (Bos grunniens) has larger lungs and heart than cattle to adapt to high altitudes.
examUse: Statement-based question on physiological adaptations of high-altitude animals.
distractorHooks: Wild Yak is known as the ship of the Himalayas

claimText: The first-ever chromosome-level genome assembly has been successfully conducted for the Indian/Domestic Yak.
evidenceExcerpt: Discusses the first-ever chromosome-level genome assembly of the Indian/Domestic Yak.
examUse: Current affairs statement-based question on genetic research and biodiversity.
distractorHooks: First genome assembly of the Wild Yak
Genome assembly of the Himalayan Tahr
